qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated chat websocket sessions can attach to and steer arbitrary waiting executions
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is an authenticated Merge-node SQL Query arbitrary file-write flaw. This finding is an unauthenticated `/chat` websocket ownership bypass and does not involve the Merge node, SQL Query mode, or a filesystem write sink.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Dynamic node parameter endpoints execute node methods with attacker-supplied credential IDs
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.vulnerability_type: authorization bypass / insecure direct object reference
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory centers on Merge-node SQL Query mode writing arbitrary files. This finding is an IDOR in dynamic parameter credential resolution and third-party API use, with no Merge-node SQL path or server-side file-write primitive.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Public API exposes project variable values across project boundaries
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory concerns Merge-node SQL Query arbitrary file write leading to RCE. This finding is a cross-project variable disclosure in the public API handler and lacks the Merge-node SQL logic, file-write sink, and equivalent root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's Merge-node SQL Query arbitrary file-write root cause or an equivalent file-write-to-RCE path.
evidence: The advisory describes an authenticated vulnerability in the Merge node's SQL Query mode that lets workflow creators or editors write arbitrary files to the n8n server filesystem.
The `chat_execution_hijack` finding is an unauthenticated `/chat` websocket execution-ownership bypass and does not describe Merge-node SQL handling or filesystem writes.
The `dynamic_node_parameters_credential_idor` finding is a credential authorization flaw in dynamic parameter endpoints and does not describe arbitrary file creation or overwrite on the server.
The `public_api_project_variable_disclosure` finding is a cross-project variable read through the public API and does not describe the advisory's Merge-node SQL file-write mechanism.
